mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate declines are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or threshold crossing fails to predict direction with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of co-expansion periods over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during industrial production uptrends are negative in > 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when filtered for early-cycle expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold fails to separate winning and losing periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during payroll growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals underlying demand resilience that markets initially misprice, correcting with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for earnings seasonality.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply chain bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during industrial production declines in late-cycle regimes are positive in > 60% of cases, or the signal fails to predict direction when filtered for late-cycle conditions.
